calibration_correction.evidence_anchor: operation set 20260704-130224-experiment-7f255a5e: calibration_params.json and calibrate.py calibrate_combined

caveats: Ground-truth runtimes were produced by a documented high-fidelity white-box emulator, not by real B200 kernel launches; silicon validation would strengthen the finding.
The combined model calibrates four factors, not six; l2_factor and tma_factor values reported for the combined model in the previous draft were actually from the TMA/L2 ablation.
The validation matrix is synthetic and covers batch, heads, seqlen, head_dim, causal, and precision only.

citation_resolution_plan.output: A references.bib file with complete BibTeX entries and inline LaTeX/Markdown citations in Related Work.
sources_to_resolve: FlashAttention-3 paper (Shah et al., NeurIPS 2024 / arXiv:2407.08608)
FlashAttention-1 paper (Dao et al., NeurIPS 2022 / arXiv:2205.14135)
Hierarchical Roofline for GPUs (Yang, Kurth & Williams, Concurrency and Computation 2019)
Microbenchmark-driven analytical GPU modeling (Jarmusch & Chandrasekaran, arXiv:2605.04178v1)

sections.claim_update: The combined white-box predictor is supported. The calibration claim is narrowed: the combined model searches four factors (hbm_factor, mma_efficiency, mufu_efficiency, launch_overhead_us) while l2_factor, tma_factor, and smem_factor are fixed at defaults (0.70, 0.65, 0.90). The TMA/L2 ablation separately calibrates l2_factor=0.45 and tma_factor=0.70.

figure_generation_plan.figure_1: Predicted vs measured runtime scatter for the combined model on the 160-config validation set, with identity line and 30% error bands. Source: combined_predictions.csv.
figure_generation_plan.figure_2: Residual distribution by predicted bottleneck (TMA, HBM, MMA, MUFU) for the combined model. Source: combined_predictions.csv.
figure_generation_plan.figure_3: Predictor pipeline diagram: input configuration -> algorithm quantities -> baseline roofline -> occupancy / effective bandwidth / precision corrections -> calibration -> predicted runtime and bottleneck label. Source: predictor.py and calibrate.py logic.

key_findings: Combined model MAPE is 4.50% versus the baseline 22.22%, an improvement of 17.72 percentage points.
All 160 held-out validation configurations are within 30% absolute error.
Bottleneck-label accuracy is 100%; the predicted dominant bottleneck matches the emulator on every config.
The TMA/L2 effective-bandwidth ablation alone achieves 5.76% MAPE, confirming bandwidth modeling is the largest single error source in the baseline roofline.
Occupancy and precision terms, though small individually, refine the combined model from 5.76% to 4.50% MAPE and reduce max APE from 19.91% to 14.36%.
next_actions: Update the draft with the corrected calibration description in Sections 3.3, 3.4, and 5.6.
Render Figures 1-3 from combined_predictions.csv and embed them in the revised draft.
Resolve Related Work citation placeholders and attach a complete references.bib.
Run manuscript validation and route to finalize if all checks pass.
sections.research_question: Does the revised draft accurately describe which bounded factors are calibrated in the combined model, and can the existing evidence support corrected prose, a complete bibliography, and three rendered figures?
sections.route_reason: The empirical evidence already supports the combined predictor. The revision pass only needs text corrections, real citations, and rendered figures, so the analysis stage routes to isomer-deepsci-write rather than to new experiments.

summary: Updated interpretation for the revision pass: the combined predictor remains supported, but the calibration description is corrected to four searched factors plus fixed defaults. The analysis also authorizes a figure-generation and citation-resolution writeback to the draft.
title: Analysis Campaign Summary: FA4 B200 White-Box Runtime Model (Revision-Pass Update)
